rubric: Defines 'AI self-evolution' with at least 3 distinct sub-concepts (e.g., recursive self-improvement, self-modifying architecture, automated ML optimization)
Lists at least 5 foundational papers or methods that define the AI self-evolution field (e.g., STaR, RLHF, Constitutional AI, Self-Play, Reflexion)
Covers at least 3 distinct mechanisms of self-evolution (e.g., prompt optimization, weight update loops, skill library expansion, reward model evolution)
Lists at least 5 key academic papers from 2025-2026 specifically on AI self-evolution or closely related self-improvement systems
Identifies at least 3 open-source projects or frameworks explicitly supporting AI self-evolution or self-improvement loops
Mentions at least 3 commercial companies or products building self-evolving AI systems (e.g., startups, enterprise platforms)
Covers Chinese community perspectives with at least 2 sources from Chinese platforms (Zhihu, CSDN, Juejin, WeChat, InfoQ-CN)
Addresses safety and alignment concerns specific to self-evolving AI with at least 2 named risks (e.g., reward hacking, capability gain without alignment, specification gaming)
Provides at least 1 concrete production or near-production example of AI self-evolution in a real system
Distinguishes between at least 2 levels of self-evolution (e.g., prompt/workflow level vs weight/architecture level)
Names at least 3 key research labs or institutions active in AI self-evolution research (e.g., Google DeepMind, Sakana AI, Stanford, etc.)
Covers at least 1 benchmark or evaluation framework used to measure AI self-evolution progress
Mentions at least 1 workshop or conference venue specifically dedicated to self-evolving or self-improving AI (NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML workshops)
Includes at least 2 items from 2026 (papers, releases, or announcements) to confirm freshness
Covers the DSPy self-optimization or prompt-optimization dimension of AI self-evolution
Provides a taxonomy or classification framework organizing AI self-evolution approaches into at least 3 distinct categories
Compares at least 3 self-evolution approaches and identifies at least 1 concrete tradeoff for each
Identifies at least 1 directional trend shift in the field between 2024 and 2026 with evidence from multiple sources
Provides causal reasoning for at least 1 major pattern — why it emerged, not just that it exists
States at least 1 concrete recommendation for practitioners (which approach fits which use case)
Identifies at least 1 open controversy or unresolved debate in the field with arguments on both sides
Discusses the safety-capability tradeoff in self-evolving systems with at least 1 alignment framework or governance approach
Identifies what Chinese AI community contributes uniquely vs Western academic approaches (not just translation of English content)
Includes an explicit gap declaration section listing at least 2 areas where evidence was insufficient or absent
Every major claim about a specific paper, project, or company has an inline citation from search results
Uses sources from at least 4 distinct source types (academic, open-source, commercial, community)
Clearly distinguishes between [background knowledge] items and [discovered] items from search
Includes a structured evidence appendix with all search results and their relevance scores
